mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60-day windows in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during a post-recession recovery phase reflect expanding factory utilization and capacity, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day forward returns in > 50% of observations during confirmed recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined positive growth signal in PAYEMS and GDPC1 produces a negative return frequency above 45% over 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real personal consumption expenditures during a late-cycle expansion signal weakening household balance sheets, which directly reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate improvements in manufacturing output and supply chain throughput, generating short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns with a frequency exceeding 52% in out-of-sample testing.
